content.text: **BUILD SUCCESSFUL**, тесты зелёные (5/5 + 7/7). Зафиксирую раскладку в память, чтобы следующие BLE-задачи (connect) не разошлись снова — гляну текущий файл:

content.content:
1	---
2	name: kmp-shared-vm-stack
3	description: "radiacode-kmp BLE/device-core port — decisions + status (prototyped end-to-end, then stashed to restart incrementally)"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 58543058-e618-4a70-9ad9-5c98ea0d59e2
8	---
9	
10	## Status (2026-06)
11	The whole BLE/device core was prototyped end-to-end (scan; shared KMP-OVM ViewModel consumed by both SwiftUI and Compose; Kable transport; `DeviceConnection`/connect) and verified to **build on Android + iOS** — then **rolled back to a git stash** to restart smaller/incrementally. Working tree = clean KMP skeleton (Greeting/Platform).
12	Recover the reference impl: `git stash list` → entry **"BLE prototype — rolled back to restart incrementally"** → `git stash show -p stash@{N}`. **Pull pieces out by hand as needed; do NOT `pop` wholesale** — we go piece-by-piece.
13	
14	## Migration method (agreed with Matvei)
15	- Single-source: shared logic → `commonMain` (Kotlin); both platforms consume; iOS refactored onto it.
16	- **Minimal PRs, bottom-up** (leaf pure logic → … → features), **PROD-FIRST**: each PR = shared code + production app wired to it + Swift duplicate deleted. No dead shared code, no throwaway test screens.
17	- Keep iOS diffs tiny via Swift `typealias Old = Shared.New`. Always green/shippable both platforms.
18	- Naming decided **per-piece when porting**, not all upfront.
19	- 1 PR = 1 branch `Feature/RMP-XXXX` = 1 commit, base `develop`, reviewer `vxrossa`.
20	
21	## Persistence = separate LATE track (do NOT port persisted types as leaves)
22	- DB = SwiftData, 10 schema versions (SwiftData → KMP SQLDelight/Room is its own heavy track).
23	- **Only DB-coupled device type = `Alarm.Level`** (Codable inside `EventDataObject.AlarmData`) → defer.
24	- Device identity/model are stored as **strings** (`deviceId`/`deviceModel`) and reconstructed at runtime — NOT persisted as structs → safe to port. Watch model-name **string parity** (Zero `displayName` "RadiaCode-Zero" vs advertised "RadiaCode Zero").
25	- Calibration stored as `[Float]` (not the struct); signal/config/firmware/settings are transient → all safe.
26	
27	## Tech choices (verified by building)
28	- **BLE = Kable** `com.juul.kable:kable-core:0.40.0` in `commonMain` (one impl serves Android AND iOS). coroutines `1.10.2`.
29	- **Shared VMs = KMP-ObservableViewModel** (`com.rickclephas.kmp`; Gradle plugin `com.rickclephas.kmp.nativecoroutines` ~1.0.3, dep `kmp-observableviewmodel-core` ~1.0.4). Chosen over **SKIE** (SKIE supported Kotlin only up to ~2.3.10; project is 2.3.21). SwiftUI: `@StateViewModel` + retroactive `Observable` conformance on `Kmp_observableviewmodel_coreViewModel` = the Observation path (iOS 17+). iOS already has a Swift `DeviceListViewModel` → reference shared one as `Shared.DeviceListViewModel`. Module-wide opt-ins in build.gradle: ExperimentalObjCName, ExperimentalForeignApi, ExperimentalUuidApi.
30	- **Kable connect API** (verified via `javap` of cached `kable-core-debug-runtime.jar`): `Peripheral(advertisement)`; `peripheral.connect()/disconnect()`; `peripheral.services.value` (`DiscoveredService.serviceUuid` / `.characteristics`); characteristic props via EXTENSION properties imported by name from `com.juul.kable` (`write`/`writeWithoutResponse`/`notify`/`indicate` on `Characteristic.Properties`); `peripheral.write(char, bytes, WriteType.WithResponse)`; `peripheral.observe(char): Flow<ByteArray>`; `peripheral.state`. Service UUID `E63215E5-7003-49D8-96B0-B024798FB901`; scan filters by it.
31	
32	## Device-core target (kept deliberately simple)
33	- `DeviceConnection` (platform, holds the handle): `write(packets)`, `notifications: Flow<ByteArray>`, `state`, `disconnect()`. **BYTES ONLY — must know nothing about commands / RawStorage / isLastPackage.** (iOS `Peripheral` today is generic over `BluetoothCommand` and owns RawStorage + accumulation — a layering smell; when adapted to `DeviceConnection` it gets STRIPPED to a dumb byte pipe and that logic moves up to `DeviceClient`.)
34	- `DeviceClient` (commonMain) = the command-send manager + queue: `suspend send<R>(command): R`, serialized via `Mutex` (one command in-flight). Loop: frame → write → accumulate notifications into the command's RawStorage until `isLastPackage` → decode. ALL command/codec knowledge lives here, NOT in the transport.
35	- Commands know their own bytes (codec, pure Kotlin).
36	- Transport split is per-platform behind `DeviceConnection`: iOS keeps its proven Swift `Peripheral` (CoreBluetooth) as an adapter (don't rewrite/replace with Kable hastily — hardware-parity risk); Android = Kable. `DeviceTransmition` (iOS) → replaced by shared `DeviceClient`.
37	- `RadiaCode` = ONE data entity, **enriched after connect**; carries NO transport handle. Bundle enriched data (firmware/settings/alarms/calibration/config) in ONE optional (e.g. `info: DeviceInfo?`) to avoid a pile of nullable fields. Scan-result and identity collapsed for now. Names `DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` were rejected by Matvei — lean to a single `RadiaCode`.
38	- `ConnectionController` + `DeviceSetup` (handshake) + Repositories (polling→Flows) + VMs — all just USERS of `send`; build later.
39	
40	## First prod-first piece (planned)
41	iOS advertisement-name parsing → shared parser (pure strings; single-source; the Android transport already used it).
42	
43	## Codec ground-truth (from iOS `Services/BluetoothCommands/*`; all little-endian)
44	Request: `[type?4][op4][if writeVirtualString → len4, or single 0x00 when no payload][payload?]`, then `[LE u32 = len(data)] ++ data`, chunked by 18 bytes (ONE length word, then chunk). Response framing: Default storage (header excl. leading length word = 8 or 12; `isValid == 1`) vs System (header 4, no valid key). Per-command decoders; CP1251 strings. SetExchange hostID `0xFF12FF01`.
45	
46	## Jira
47	Epics: RMP-6 [Core] BLE Protocol, RMP-7 [iOS] BLE Protocol, RMP-8 [Android] BLE Protocol. RMP-1 [Core] Shared (DI = Koin, persistence = SQLDelight/Room). See [[pr-flow-radiacode-kmp]] for branch/PR rules.
48

file.content:
---
name: kmp-shared-vm-stack
description: "radiacode-kmp BLE/device-core port — decisions + status (prototyped end-to-end, then stashed to restart incrementally)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 58543058-e618-4a70-9ad9-5c98ea0d59e2
---

## Status (2026-06)
The whole BLE/device core was prototyped end-to-end (scan; shared KMP-OVM ViewModel consumed by both SwiftUI and Compose; Kable transport; `DeviceConnection`/connect) and verified to **build on Android + iOS** — then **rolled back to a git stash** to restart smaller/incrementally. Working tree = clean KMP skeleton (Greeting/Platform).
Recover the reference impl: `git stash list` → entry **"BLE prototype — rolled back to restart incrementally"** → `git stash show -p stash@{N}`. **Pull pieces out by hand as needed; do NOT `pop` wholesale** — we go piece-by-piece.

## Migration method (agreed with Matvei)
- Single-source: shared logic → `commonMain` (Kotlin); both platforms consume; iOS refactored onto it.
- **Minimal PRs, bottom-up** (leaf pure logic → … → features), **PROD-FIRST**: each PR = shared code + production app wired to it + Swift duplicate deleted. No dead shared code, no throwaway test screens.
- Keep iOS diffs tiny via Swift `typealias Old = Shared.New`. Always green/shippable both platforms.
- Naming decided **per-piece when porting**, not all upfront.
- 1 PR = 1 branch `Feature/RMP-XXXX` = 1 commit, base `develop`, reviewer `vxrossa`.

## Persistence = separate LATE track (do NOT port persisted types as leaves)
- DB = SwiftData, 10 schema versions (SwiftData → KMP SQLDelight/Room is its own heavy track).
- **Only DB-coupled device type = `Alarm.Level`** (Codable inside `EventDataObject.AlarmData`) → defer.
- Device identity/model are stored as **strings** (`deviceId`/`deviceModel`) and reconstructed at runtime — NOT persisted as structs → safe to port. Watch model-name **string parity** (Zero `displayName` "RadiaCode-Zero" vs advertised "RadiaCode Zero").
- Calibration stored as `[Float]` (not the struct); signal/config/firmware/settings are transient → all safe.

## Tech choices (verified by building)
- **BLE = Kable** `com.juul.kable:kable-core:0.40.0` in `commonMain` (one impl serves Android AND iOS). coroutines `1.10.2`.
- **Shared VMs = KMP-ObservableViewModel** (`com.rickclephas.kmp`; Gradle plugin `com.rickclephas.kmp.nativecoroutines` ~1.0.3, dep `kmp-observableviewmodel-core` ~1.0.4). Chosen over **SKIE** (SKIE supported Kotlin only up to ~2.3.10; project is 2.3.21). SwiftUI: `@StateViewModel` + retroactive `Observable` conformance on `Kmp_observableviewmodel_coreViewModel` = the Observation path (iOS 17+). iOS already has a Swift `DeviceListViewModel` → reference shared one as `Shared.DeviceListViewModel`. Module-wide opt-ins in build.gradle: ExperimentalObjCName, ExperimentalForeignApi, ExperimentalUuidApi.
- **Kable connect API** (verified via `javap` of cached `kable-core-debug-runtime.jar`): `Peripheral(advertisement)`; `peripheral.connect()/disconnect()`; `peripheral.services.value` (`DiscoveredService.serviceUuid` / `.characteristics`); characteristic props via EXTENSION properties imported by name from `com.juul.kable` (`write`/`writeWithoutResponse`/`notify`/`indicate` on `Characteristic.Properties`); `peripheral.write(char, bytes, WriteType.WithResponse)`; `peripheral.observe(char): Flow<ByteArray>`; `peripheral.state`. Service UUID `E63215E5-7003-49D8-96B0-B024798FB901`; scan filters by it.

## Device-core target (kept deliberately simple)
- `DeviceConnection` (platform, holds the handle): `write(packets)`, `notifications: Flow<ByteArray>`, `state`, `disconnect()`. **BYTES ONLY — must know nothing about commands / RawStorage / isLastPackage.** (iOS `Peripheral` today is generic over `BluetoothCommand` and owns RawStorage + accumulation — a layering smell; when adapted to `DeviceConnection` it gets STRIPPED to a dumb byte pipe and that logic moves up to `DeviceClient`.)
- `DeviceClient` (commonMain) = the command-send manager + queue: `suspend send<R>(command): R`, serialized via `Mutex` (one command in-flight). Loop: frame → write → accumulate notifications into the command's RawStorage until `isLastPackage` → decode. ALL command/codec knowledge lives here, NOT in the transport.
- Commands know their own bytes (codec, pure Kotlin).
- Transport split is per-platform behind `DeviceConnection`: iOS keeps its proven Swift `Peripheral` (CoreBluetooth) as an adapter (don't rewrite/replace with Kable hastily — hardware-parity risk); Android = Kable. `DeviceTransmition` (iOS) → replaced by shared `DeviceClient`.
- `RadiaCode` = ONE data entity, **enriched after connect**; carries NO transport handle. Bundle enriched data (firmware/settings/alarms/calibration/config) in ONE optional (e.g. `info: DeviceInfo?`) to avoid a pile of nullable fields. Scan-result and identity collapsed for now. Names `DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` were rejected by Matvei — lean to a single `RadiaCode`.
- `ConnectionController` + `DeviceSetup` (handshake) + Repositories (polling→Flows) + VMs — all just USERS of `send`; build later.

## First prod-first piece (planned)
iOS advertisement-name parsing → shared parser (pure strings; single-source; the Android transport already used it).

## Codec ground-truth (from iOS `Services/BluetoothCommands/*`; all little-endian)
Request: `[type?4][op4][if writeVirtualString → len4, or single 0x00 when no payload][payload?]`, then `[LE u32 = len(data)] ++ data`, chunked by 18 bytes (ONE length word, then chunk). Response framing: Default storage (header excl. leading length word = 8 or 12; `isValid == 1`) vs System (header 4, no valid key). Per-command decoders; CP1251 strings. SetExchange hostID `0xFF12FF01`.

## Jira
Epics: RMP-6 [Core] BLE Protocol, RMP-7 [iOS] BLE Protocol, RMP-8 [Android] BLE Protocol. RMP-1 [Core] Shared (DI = Koin, persistence = SQLDelight/Room). See [[pr-flow-radiacode-kmp]] for branch/PR rules.

Names `DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` were rejected by Matvei — lean to a single `RadiaCode`.

So identity and scan-find are TWO types (Discovery holds a Device), not collapsed. (`DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` rejected; `RadiaCodeDiscovery` accepted.)

input.old_string:
## First prod-first piece (planned)
iOS advertisement-name parsing → shared parser (pure strings; single-source; the Android transport already used it).
input.new_string:
## Package layout (`core/device/`, agreed RMP-23)
`model/` data · `scan/` `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` + `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` · `connection/` `DeviceConnection`+connect (later). Scanner is named **`DeviceScanner`** (NOT `BleScanner`) to match the `DeviceConnection`/`DeviceClient` family. Parser is its own unit (`object RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser`, internal) NOT a method on the model — model stays pure data; parser kept testable in isolation (decided: yes, unit-test the parser).

## Done so far
- **RMP-26:** model layer (`core/device/model/`) + tests. PR #8.
- **RMP-23 (shared-only):** scan layer — `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` (Kable, filter by service UUID) → `Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>`; pure `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` (port of `didDiscover`, 5 unit tests). Deps added: coroutines + kable ONLY; opt-in ONLY `ExperimentalUuidApi` (ObjCName/ForeignApi/nativecoroutines/observableviewmodel come back WITH the VM). NO connect / VM / UI / manifest perms (deferred). The old "first prod-first piece" (name parsing) landed here.
- Gradle needs `JAVA_HOME=/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home` (Java not on PATH); verify with `./gradlew :shared:assemble :shared:allTests`.

Готово — выровнено под RMP-26.

**Итог переноса (RMP-23, shared-only):**

`shared/.../core/device/`
- `model/` — без изменений (чистые данные из RMP-26)
- `scan/` ← новое:
  - [DeviceScanner.kt](shared/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/DeviceScanner.kt) — `RadiaCodeBle` + `interface DeviceScanner` + `KableDeviceScanner`
  - [RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser.kt](shared/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser.kt) — `internal object`, порт `didDiscover`
  - [RadiaCodeDiscoveryParserTest.kt](shared/src/commonTest/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/RadiaCodeDiscoveryParserTest.kt) — 5/5

Сборка **SUCCESSFUL**, тесты 5/5 + 7/7.

**Что подтвердил поиск в RMP-26:** отдельный парсер + покрытие его тестами — это решение там уже принималось (*«чистый парсер — его и покроем тестами»*), так что мы не разошлись, а попали в то же русло. Заодно выровнял нейминг (`DeviceScanner`) и раскладку (`scan/`) под тогдашние наброски, и обновил память [[kmp-shared-vm-stack]] разделом про раскладку пакетов и статусом RMP-23 — чтобы будущий connect лёг в `core/device/connection/` без новых расхождений.

Готов оформить коммит/ветку, когда скажешь базу.

Готово, всё зелёное (5/5 + 7/7).

- [RadiaCodeBle.kt](shared/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/RadiaCodeBle.kt) — новый файл: `SERVICE_UUID` + `DEFAULT_NAME` («RadiaCode», префикс BLE-имени).
- [DeviceScanner.kt](shared/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/DeviceScanner.kt) — остался только интерфейс + Kable-реализация.

(Это правка модельного файла из RMP-26 — она попадёт в дифф RMP-23, имей в виду.)

Один нюанс на будущее: `RadiaCodeBle` лежит в `scan/`, но `SERVICE_UUID` понадобится и connect-слою (поиск характеристик). Когда появится `connection/`, константы можно поднять на уровень `core/device/` — решим там, по нашему правилу «покусочно».

lastPrompt: в ios у нас используются PeripheralAdvertisementKeys, сейчас они не нужны?

content.text:
Правильно — не нужны. Вот почему:

**Что это в iOS.** `PeripheralAdvertisementKeys` — это строковые ключи словаря CoreBluetooth: в `didDiscover` система отдаёт сырой `advertisementData: [String: Any]`, и значения оттуда достаются вручную по ключам вида `"kCBAdvDataLocalName"`. Причём из семи ключей в этом enum реально используется только один — `localName` в [BluetoothManager.swift:163](iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Device/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.swift:163); остальные шесть (timestamp, connectable, PHY, manufacturer, serviceUUIDs) уже сейчас мёртвые.

**Почему у нас их нет.** Этот слой существует только потому, что CoreBluetooth отдаёт нетипизированный словарь. Kable разбирает его сам и наружу отдаёт типизированный `Advertisement`: его `.name` — это и есть значение за `kCBAdvDataLocalName`, а `.peripheralName` — `CBPeripheral.name`. Наш `KableDeviceScanner` берёт уже готовые поля, а парсер получает чистые строки. Соответствие зафиксировано в KDoc `parse` (`@param localName — iOS kCBAdvDataLocalName, Android Advertisement.name`), так что связь с iOS-ключом не потеряна, она просто спрятана внутри Kable.

**Что с ними будет.** Когда iOS переедет на общий сканер, `DeviceBluetoothConstants` со всем enum уйдёт вместе с Swift-сканом (наш принцип «перенёс → удалил Swift-дубль»). Тащить ключи в shared не нужно ни сейчас, ни потом.

fields.description:
Слой BLE-поиска приборов в `shared/commonMain` (`core/device/scan`) поверх моделей из RMP-26: контракт `DeviceScanner` → `Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>` (холодный поток — подписка запускает поиск, отмена останавливает; дедупликация на потребителе), реализация `KableDeviceScanner` (Kable, общая для Android и iOS, фильтр по сервису RadiaCode), парсер BLE-имени `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` (порт `BluetoothManager.didDiscover`, покрыт юнит-тестами).

**Зависимости:** kotlinx-coroutines 1.10.2, Kable 0.40.0.

Подключение, статус адаптера, ViewModel и UI — отдельными задачами.

**Зависимости:** kotlinx-coroutines 1.10.2, Kable 0.40.0.

status.name: В работе

content.text:
Обновил [RMP-23](https://radiacode.atlassian.net/browse/RMP-23):

- **Заголовок:** «DeviceScanner (сканер приборов RadiaCode)» — был «BluetoothManager (поиск, выдача, подключение)».
- **Описание:** три абзаца — что за слой и из чего состоит (контракт → `Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>`, Kable-реализация, парсер с тестами), зависимости, и что подключение/статус адаптера/VM/UI — отдельные задачи.

Будущий коммит по правилам репо получится `RMP-23 DeviceScanner (сканер приборов RadiaCode)`. Когда решишь с веткой/базой — оформлю.

gh pr create --base develop --title "RMP-23 DeviceScanner (сканер приборов RadiaCode)" --reviewer vxrossa --body "RMP-23 — https://radiacode.atlassian.net/browse/RMP-23

## shared (commonMain) — слой BLE-поиска приборов (\`core/device/scan\`)
- **DeviceScanner** — контракт: \`scan(): Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>\`; холодный поток — подписка запускает поиск, отмена останавливает; дедупликация на потребителе
- **KableDeviceScanner** — реализация поверх Kable (одна на Android и iOS), фильтр по сервису RadiaCode
- **RadiaCodeBle** — BLE-константы (\`SERVICE_UUID\`, \`DEFAULT_NAME\`; \`DEFAULT_NAME\` переехал сюда из \`RadiaCodeModel\`)
- **RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser** — разбор BLE-имени (порт \`BluetoothManager.didDiscover\`): имя из \`name\`/\`localName\`, префикс \`RadiaCode\`, id из \`RadiaCode-XXX#<id>\`, иначе \`peripheralId\`
- **RadiaCodeDiscoveryParserTest** — 5 тестов (зелёные на \`iosSimulatorArm64\`)

## Зависимости
- kotlinx-coroutines 1.10.2, Kable 0.40.0; opt-in \`ExperimentalUuidApi\`

Подключение, статус адаптера, ViewModel и UI — отдельными задачами."

Готово, допушил в [PR #15](https://github.com/Radiacode/radiacode-kmp/pull/15) (`00e0d4ca`).

Саджест с переименованием объекта оставил ветку несобираемой — файл и четыре ссылки ещё помнили старое имя. Доделал:
- файл → `RadiacodeBLEParams.kt` (через `git mv`, история сохранена);
- ссылки: `SERVICE_UUID` в [DeviceScanner.kt:23](shared/src/commonMain/kotlin/com/radiacode/shared/core/device/scan/DeviceScanner.kt:23) и три `DEFAULT_NAME` в парсере.

Сборка зелёная, тесты 5/5 + 7/7. В ветке теперь три коммита — при сквош-мердже PR схлопнутся в один, так что правило «одна ветка — один коммит» не пострадает.

done

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: kmp-shared-vm-stack
description: "radiacode-kmp BLE/device-core port — decisions + status (prototyped end-to-end, then stashed to restart incrementally)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 58543058-e618-4a70-9ad9-5c98ea0d59e2
---

## Status (2026-06)
The whole BLE/device core was prototyped end-to-end (scan; shared KMP-OVM ViewModel consumed by both SwiftUI and Compose; Kable transport; `DeviceConnection`/connect) and verified to **build on Android + iOS** — then **rolled back to a git stash** to restart smaller/incrementally. Working tree = clean KMP skeleton (Greeting/Platform).
Recover the reference impl: `git stash list` → entry **"BLE prototype — rolled back to restart incrementally"** → `git stash show -p stash@{N}`. **Pull pieces out by hand as needed; do NOT `pop` wholesale** — we go piece-by-piece.

## Migration method (agreed with Matvei)
- Single-source: shared logic → `commonMain` (Kotlin); both platforms consume; iOS refactored onto it.
- **Minimal PRs, bottom-up** (leaf pure logic → … → features), **PROD-FIRST**: each PR = shared code + production app wired to it + Swift duplicate deleted. No dead shared code, no throwaway test screens.
- Keep iOS diffs tiny via Swift `typealias Old = Shared.New`. Always green/shippable both platforms.
- Naming decided **per-piece when porting**, not all upfront.
- 1 PR = 1 branch `Feature/RMP-XXXX` = 1 commit, base `develop`, reviewer `vxrossa`.

## Persistence = separate LATE track (do NOT port persisted types as leaves)
- DB = SwiftData, 10 schema versions (SwiftData → KMP SQLDelight/Room is its own heavy track).
- **Only DB-coupled device type = `Alarm.Level`** (Codable inside `EventDataObject.AlarmData`) → defer.
- Device identity/model are stored as **strings** (`deviceId`/`deviceModel`) and reconstructed at runtime — NOT persisted as structs → safe to port. Watch model-name **string parity** (Zero `displayName` "RadiaCode-Zero" vs advertised "RadiaCode Zero").
- Calibration stored as `[Float]` (not the struct); signal/config/firmware/settings are transient → all safe.

## Tech choices (verified by building)
- **BLE = Kable** `com.juul.kable:kable-core:0.40.0` in `commonMain` (one impl serves Android AND iOS). coroutines `1.10.2`.
- **Shared VMs = KMP-ObservableViewModel** (`com.rickclephas.kmp`; Gradle plugin `com.rickclephas.kmp.nativecoroutines` ~1.0.3, dep `kmp-observableviewmodel-core` ~1.0.4). Chosen over **SKIE** (SKIE supported Kotlin only up to ~2.3.10; project is 2.3.21). SwiftUI: `@StateViewModel` + retroactive `Observable` conformance on `Kmp_observableviewmodel_coreViewModel` = the Observation path (iOS 17+). iOS already has a Swift `DeviceListViewModel` → reference shared one as `Shared.DeviceListViewModel`. Module-wide opt-ins in build.gradle: ExperimentalObjCName, ExperimentalForeignApi, ExperimentalUuidApi.
- **Kable connect API** (verified via `javap` of cached `kable-core-debug-runtime.jar`): `Peripheral(advertisement)`; `peripheral.connect()/disconnect()`; `peripheral.services.value` (`DiscoveredService.serviceUuid` / `.characteristics`); characteristic props via EXTENSION properties imported by name from `com.juul.kable` (`write`/`writeWithoutResponse`/`notify`/`indicate` on `Characteristic.Properties`); `peripheral.write(char, bytes, WriteType.WithResponse)`; `peripheral.observe(char): Flow<ByteArray>`; `peripheral.state`. Service UUID `E63215E5-7003-49D8-96B0-B024798FB901`; scan filters by it.

## Device-core target (kept deliberately simple)
- `DeviceConnection` (platform, holds the handle): `write(packets)`, `notifications: Flow<ByteArray>`, `state`, `disconnect()`. **BYTES ONLY — must know nothing about commands / RawStorage / isLastPackage.** (iOS `Peripheral` today is generic over `BluetoothCommand` and owns RawStorage + accumulation — a layering smell; when adapted to `DeviceConnection` it gets STRIPPED to a dumb byte pipe and that logic moves up to `DeviceClient`.)
- `DeviceClient` (commonMain) = the command-send manager + queue: `suspend send<R>(command): R`, serialized via `Mutex` (one command in-flight). Loop: frame → write → accumulate notifications into the command's RawStorage until `isLastPackage` → decode. ALL command/codec knowledge lives here, NOT in the transport.
- Commands know their own bytes (codec, pure Kotlin).
- Transport split is per-platform behind `DeviceConnection`: iOS keeps its proven Swift `Peripheral` (CoreBluetooth) as an adapter (don't rewrite/replace with Kable hastily — hardware-parity risk); Android = Kable. `DeviceTransmition` (iOS) → replaced by shared `DeviceClient`.
- **Models (DONE, RMP-26, `core/device/model/`):** `RadiaCodeDevice(identifier, model)` identity (`==` by identifier) · `RadiaCodeModel` (sealed; `invoke(rawName)` / `serialCode`) · `RadiaCodeFamily` · `BluetoothSignal` (`invoke(rssi: Int?)`) · `RadiaCodeDiscovery(device, signal, peripheralId)` = scan find, `==` by device (NOT a data class), `peripheralId` = Kable `adv.identifier` for connect/reconnect. So identity and scan-find are TWO types (Discovery holds a Device), not collapsed. (`DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` rejected; `RadiaCodeDiscovery` accepted.)
- **Connected device** (later, at connect): a `RadiaCode`-ish entity enriched after connect, NO transport handle, enriched data in ONE optional bundle (e.g. `info: DeviceInfo?`). Final shape decided at connect time.
- `ConnectionController` + `DeviceSetup` (handshake) + Repositories (polling→Flows) + VMs — all just USERS of `send`; build later.

## Package layout (`core/device/`, agreed RMP-23)
`model/` data · `scan/` `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` + `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` · `connection/` `DeviceConnection`+connect (later). Scanner is named **`DeviceScanner`** (NOT `BleScanner`) to match the `DeviceConnection`/`DeviceClient` family. Parser is its own unit (`object RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser`, internal) NOT a method on the model — model stays pure data; parser kept testable in isolation (decided: yes, unit-test the parser).

## Done so far
- **RMP-26:** model layer (`core/device/model/`) + tests. PR #8.
- **RMP-23 (shared-only):** scan layer — `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` (Kable, filter by service UUID) → `Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>`; pure `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` (port of `didDiscover`, 5 unit tests). Deps added: coroutines + kable ONLY; opt-in ONLY `ExperimentalUuidApi` (ObjCName/ForeignApi/nativecoroutines/observableviewmodel come back WITH the VM). NO connect / VM / UI / manifest perms (deferred). The old "first prod-first piece" (name parsing) landed here.
- Gradle needs `JAVA_HOME=/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home` (Java not on PATH); verify with `./gradlew :shared:assemble :shared:allTests`.

## Codec ground-truth (from iOS `Services/BluetoothCommands/*`; all little-endian)
Request: `[type?4][op4][if writeVirtualString → len4, or single 0x00 when no payload][payload?]`, then `[LE u32 = len(data)] ++ data`, chunked by 18 bytes (ONE length word, then chunk). Response framing: Default storage (header excl. leading length word = 8 or 12; `isValid == 1`) vs System (header 4, no valid key). Per-command decoders; CP1251 strings. SetExchange hostID `0xFF12FF01`.

## Jira
Epics: RMP-6 [Core] BLE Protocol, RMP-7 [iOS] BLE Protocol, RMP-8 [Android] BLE Protocol. RMP-1 [Core] Shared (DI = Koin, persistence = SQLDelight/Room). See [[pr-flow-radiacode-kmp]] for branch/PR rules.

lastPrompt: можешь ли еще обновить либы до последних

content.text:
Обновил и запушил (`7b3dc28c` в [PR #15](https://github.com/Radiacode/radiacode-kmp/pull/15)):

- **kotlinx-coroutines** `1.10.2 → 1.11.0` — последняя стабильная.
- **Kable** `0.40.0 → 0.43.0` — это потолок, а не последняя: свежайшая `0.43.1` собрана **Kotlin 2.4.0**, и её klib наш Kotlin 2.3.21 читать отказывается (проверил сборкой и манифестами klib: 0.43.0 собран ровно 2.3.21, 0.43.1 — уже 2.4.0). Догоним её вместе с бампом Kotlin.

Сборка и тесты зелёные (5/5 + 7/7), правок в исходниках не потребовалось — API скана у Kable между 0.40 и 0.43 не менялся.

Остальное в `libs.versions.toml` (Kotlin 2.3.21, AGP, compose-bom, activity-compose) не трогал — это тулчейн и Android-приложение, вне скоупа RMP-23; если хочешь, бампнем отдельной задачей.

name: kmp-shared-vm-stack
description: "radiacode-kmp BLE/device-core port — decisions + status (prototyped end-to-end, then stashed to restart incrementally)"
metadata:

  type: project

## Status (2026-06)
The whole BLE/device core was prototyped end-to-end (scan; shared KMP-OVM ViewModel consumed by both SwiftUI and Compose; Kable transport; `DeviceConnection`/connect) and verified to **build on Android + iOS** — then **rolled back to a git stash** to restart smaller/incrementally. Working tree = clean KMP skeleton (Greeting/Platform).

- Keep iOS diffs tiny via Swift `typealias Old = Shared.New`. Always green/shippable both platforms.
- Naming decided **per-piece when porting**, not all upfront.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
name: kmp-shared-vm-stack
description: "radiacode-kmp BLE/device-core port — decisions + status (prototyped end-to-end, then stashed to restart incrementally)"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 58543058-e618-4a70-9ad9-5c98ea0d59e2
---

## Status (2026-06)
The whole BLE/device core was prototyped end-to-end (scan; shared KMP-OVM ViewModel consumed by both SwiftUI and Compose; Kable transport; `DeviceConnection`/connect) and verified to **build on Android + iOS** — then **rolled back to a git stash** to restart smaller/incrementally. Working tree = clean KMP skeleton (Greeting/Platform).
Recover the reference impl: `git stash list` → entry **"BLE prototype — rolled back to restart incrementally"** → `git stash show -p stash@{N}`. **Pull pieces out by hand as needed; do NOT `pop` wholesale** — we go piece-by-piece.

## Migration method (agreed with Matvei)
- Single-source: shared logic → `commonMain` (Kotlin); both platforms consume; iOS refactored onto it.
- **Minimal PRs, bottom-up** (leaf pure logic → … → features), **PROD-FIRST**: each PR = shared code + production app wired to it + Swift duplicate deleted. No dead shared code, no throwaway test screens.
- Keep iOS diffs tiny via Swift `typealias Old = Shared.New`. Always green/shippable both platforms.
- Naming decided **per-piece when porting**, not all upfront.
- 1 PR = 1 branch `Feature/RMP-XXXX` = 1 commit, base `develop`, reviewer `vxrossa`.

## Persistence = separate LATE track (do NOT port persisted types as leaves)
- DB = SwiftData, 10 schema versions (SwiftData → KMP SQLDelight/Room is its own heavy track).
- **Only DB-coupled device type = `Alarm.Level`** (Codable inside `EventDataObject.AlarmData`) → defer.
- Device identity/model are stored as **strings** (`deviceId`/`deviceModel`) and reconstructed at runtime — NOT persisted as structs → safe to port. Watch model-name **string parity** (Zero `displayName` "RadiaCode-Zero" vs advertised "RadiaCode Zero").
- Calibration stored as `[Float]` (not the struct); signal/config/firmware/settings are transient → all safe.

## Tech choices (verified by building)
- **BLE = Kable** `com.juul.kable:kable-core:0.43.1` in `commonMain` (one impl serves Android AND iOS). coroutines `1.11.0`. **Project Kotlin = 2.4.0** (bumped in RMP-23 PR; AGP 9.1.1 + compose fine). **Version gate when bumping KMP libs:** klib must be built with Kotlin ≤ project's, else "KLIB resolver: Could not find …" — check klib `compiler_version` (unzip klib → default/manifest).
- **Shared VMs = KMP-ObservableViewModel** (`com.rickclephas.kmp`; Gradle plugin `com.rickclephas.kmp.nativecoroutines` ~1.0.3, dep `kmp-observableviewmodel-core` ~1.0.4). Chosen over **SKIE** (SKIE supported Kotlin only up to ~2.3.10; project is 2.3.21). SwiftUI: `@StateViewModel` + retroactive `Observable` conformance on `Kmp_observableviewmodel_coreViewModel` = the Observation path (iOS 17+). iOS already has a Swift `DeviceListViewModel` → reference shared one as `Shared.DeviceListViewModel`. Module-wide opt-ins in build.gradle: ExperimentalObjCName, ExperimentalForeignApi, ExperimentalUuidApi.
- **Kable connect API** (verified via `javap` of cached `kable-core-debug-runtime.jar`): `Peripheral(advertisement)`; `peripheral.connect()/disconnect()`; `peripheral.services.value` (`DiscoveredService.serviceUuid` / `.characteristics`); characteristic props via EXTENSION properties imported by name from `com.juul.kable` (`write`/`writeWithoutResponse`/`notify`/`indicate` on `Characteristic.Properties`); `peripheral.write(char, bytes, WriteType.WithResponse)`; `peripheral.observe(char): Flow<ByteArray>`; `peripheral.state`. Service UUID `E63215E5-7003-49D8-96B0-B024798FB901`; scan filters by it.

## Device-core target (kept deliberately simple)
- `DeviceConnection` (platform, holds the handle): `write(packets)`, `notifications: Flow<ByteArray>`, `state`, `disconnect()`. **BYTES ONLY — must know nothing about commands / RawStorage / isLastPackage.** (iOS `Peripheral` today is generic over `BluetoothCommand` and owns RawStorage + accumulation — a layering smell; when adapted to `DeviceConnection` it gets STRIPPED to a dumb byte pipe and that logic moves up to `DeviceClient`.)
- `DeviceClient` (commonMain) = the command-send manager + queue: `suspend send<R>(command): R`, serialized via `Mutex` (one command in-flight). Loop: frame → write → accumulate notifications into the command's RawStorage until `isLastPackage` → decode. ALL command/codec knowledge lives here, NOT in the transport.
- Commands know their own bytes (codec, pure Kotlin).
- Transport split is per-platform behind `DeviceConnection`: iOS keeps its proven Swift `Peripheral` (CoreBluetooth) as an adapter (don't rewrite/replace with Kable hastily — hardware-parity risk); Android = Kable. `DeviceTransmition` (iOS) → replaced by shared `DeviceClient`.
- **Models (DONE, RMP-26, `core/device/model/`):** `RadiaCodeDevice(identifier, model)` identity (`==` by identifier) · `RadiaCodeModel` (sealed; `invoke(rawName)` / `serialCode`) · `RadiaCodeFamily` · `BluetoothSignal` (`invoke(rssi: Int?)`) · `RadiaCodeDiscovery(device, signal, peripheralId)` = scan find, `==` by device (NOT a data class), `peripheralId` = Kable `adv.identifier` for connect/reconnect. So identity and scan-find are TWO types (Discovery holds a Device), not collapsed. (`DiscoveredDevice`/`ConnectedDevice`/`DeviceSession` rejected; `RadiaCodeDiscovery` accepted.)
- **Connected device** (later, at connect): a `RadiaCode`-ish entity enriched after connect, NO transport handle, enriched data in ONE optional bundle (e.g. `info: DeviceInfo?`). Final shape decided at connect time.
- `ConnectionController` + `DeviceSetup` (handshake) + Repositories (polling→Flows) + VMs — all just USERS of `send`; build later.

## Package layout (`core/device/`, agreed RMP-23)
`model/` data · `scan/` `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` + `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` · `connection/` `DeviceConnection`+connect (later). Scanner is named **`DeviceScanner`** (NOT `BleScanner`) to match the `DeviceConnection`/`DeviceClient` family. Parser is its own unit (`object RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser`, internal) NOT a method on the model — model stays pure data; parser kept testable in isolation (decided: yes, unit-test the parser).

## Done so far
- **RMP-26:** model layer (`core/device/model/`) + tests. PR #8.
- **RMP-23 (shared-only):** scan layer — `DeviceScanner`/`KableDeviceScanner` (Kable, filter by service UUID) → `Flow<RadiaCodeDiscovery>`; pure `RadiaCodeDiscoveryParser` (port of `didDiscover`, 5 unit tests). Deps added: coroutines + kable ONLY; opt-in ONLY `ExperimentalUuidApi` (ObjCName/ForeignApi/nativecoroutines/observableviewmodel come back WITH the VM). NO connect / VM / UI / manifest perms (deferred). The old "first prod-first piece" (name parsing) landed here.
- Gradle needs `JAVA_HOME=/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home` (Java not on PATH); verify with `./gradlew :shared:assemble :shared:allTests`.

## Codec ground-truth (from iOS `Services/BluetoothCommands/*`; all little-endian)
Request: `[type?4][op4][if writeVirtualString → len4, or single 0x00 when no payload][payload?]`, then `[LE u32 = len(data)] ++ data`, chunked by 18 bytes (ONE length word, then chunk). Response framing: Default storage (header excl. leading length word = 8 or 12; `isValid == 1`) vs System (header 4, no valid key). Per-command decoders; CP1251 strings. SetExchange hostID `0xFF12FF01`.

## Jira
Epics: RMP-6 [Core] BLE Protocol, RMP-7 [iOS] BLE Protocol, RMP-8 [Android] BLE Protocol. RMP-1 [Core] Shared (DI = Koin, persistence = SQLDelight/Room). See [[pr-flow-radiacode-kmp]] for branch/PR rules.

content.text:
Готово — Kotlin поднят, всё в [PR #15](https://github.com/Radiacode/radiacode-kmp/pull/15) (`f26625a2`):

- **Kotlin** `2.3.21 → 2.4.0` (последняя стабильная; тянет за собой и compose-compiler плагин).
- **Kable** `0.43.0 → 0.43.1` — теперь дотянулись до настоящей последней: именно бамп Kotlin снял вчерашний блокер с klib.
- **coroutines** остаётся `1.11.0` — уже последняя.

Проверка: полная пересборка с нуля на новом тулчейне (86 задач, ~4 мин) — shared на всех таргетах, оба теста (5/5 + 7/7), **плюс `androidApp:assembleDebug`** (из-за compose-compiler), ноль варнингов.

Два примечания:
- iOS-приложение потребляет `Shared.framework` как бинарь — фреймворки слинковались, интерфейс не менялся, но при следующей сборке iosApp в Xcode фреймворк пересоберётся уже на 2.4.0 — если что-то всплывёт, это ожидаемое место.
- В памяти отметил: выбор KMP-ObservableViewModel vs SKIE делался из-за отсутствия SKIE под 2.3.21 — на 2.4.0 это надо будет перепроверить, когда дойдём до ViewModel.

lastPrompt: можешь котлин тоже поднять
